description: SPI reads return data shifted by one bit or inverted because the controller clock polarity and phase do not match the peripheral.
body: Failure mode: the controller samples on the opposite edge from where the peripheral drives data, so every bit lands in the wrong position. Detection signals: a known identity register reading as a shifted or doubled value, results that change when CPHA is toggled, and unstable reads at higher clock rates. Do not trust configuration writes while reads are misaligned. Safer fallback: set CPOL and CPHA to the datasheet values, confirm an identity-register read, and only then resume normal transactions.
